title: Ortus Raster Bundle Manifest
description: Manifest for an ortus raster bundle. Lives at the ZIP root under the fixed name 'ortus-raster.yaml'. Describes one raster source (one or more COG layers). A categorical layer maps a sampled integer pixel value to attributes (Sachdaten); a continuous layer returns the sampled value directly as a float (e.g. elevation in meters).

schema_version.description: Bundle schema version. Bump on breaking changes.

id.description: Stable source id, kebab-case. Becomes the ortus source id; must be unique across all loaded bundles.

description.description: Optional longer description of the dataset.

crs.description: Canonical CRS of every raster in this bundle. The pipeline MUST have reprojected all rasters to this CRS so ortus never reprojects at query time.

layers.description: One or more raster layers (e.g. time slices, scenarios, or tiles already mosaicked into one COG each).

name.description: SPDX id or license name, e.g. 'CC-BY-4.0'.

url.description: Link to the license or source.

layer.$comment: A layer draws pixels from exactly one of 'file' (a single COG) or 'tiles' (a degree-grid of COGs) — the allOf/oneOf enforces this. Categorical layers (the default) additionally require exactly one of 'mapping' (inline) or 'value_mapping' (sidecar). Continuous layers (value_type: continuous) carry no mapping — the sampled pixel value is returned directly as a float, scaled by scale/offset. The if/then/else enforces the mapping rule per value_type.

id.description: Layer id, kebab-case, unique within the bundle.

file.description: Relative path to the single COG inside the bundle. No leading slash; the ingest validator additionally rejects parent traversal ('..'). Mutually exclusive with 'tiles'.

nodata.description: Pixel value treated as no-data. A sample equal to nodata yields no feature (no match), not an error.

sampling.description: Resampling at the query point. Only 'nearest' is supported — categorical rasters must not interpolate (it would invent classes), and for continuous rasters nearest at native resolution is sufficient.

value_type.description: How a sampled pixel value is interpreted. 'categorical' (default) looks the integer value up in mapping/value_mapping. 'continuous' returns the value directly as a float (scaled by scale/offset) — e.g. elevation in meters. Continuous requires a numeric COG band (int8/16/32, uint8/16/32, float32/64) and must not carry mapping/value_mapping.

output_property.description: Continuous only. The Feature property name the sampled float lands in (e.g. 'meters'). Defaults to 'value'.

scale.description: Continuous only. Linear scale applied to the raw sample: out = raw*scale + offset. Default 1. Use e.g. scale 0.01 for an Int16 band stored in centimeters.

offset.description: Continuous only. Linear offset applied to the raw sample: out = raw*scale + offset. Default 0.

value_mapping.description: Relative path to a sidecar mapping file (.json/.yaml/.yml) for large tables. Same logical shape as the inline 'mapping'. No leading slash; the ingest validator additionally rejects parent traversal.

tiles.description: A multi-tile layer: many COGs on a regular degree grid, each named after its SW corner. ortus routes a query point to its grid cell, derives the tile filename from 'pattern', and samples that one tile. A point over a missing tile yields no data (ocean / no coverage). Only continuous layers use tiles.

dir.description: Relative directory inside the bundle holding the tile COGs. No leading slash; parent traversal is rejected.

pattern.description: Tile filename template. Tokens: {ns}=N|S, {ew}=E|W, {lat}=abs latitude zero-padded to 2 digits, {lon}=abs longitude zero-padded to 3 digits — all for the cell's SW corner. E.g. '{ns}{lat}_{ew}{lon}.tif' -> 'N49_E010.tif'.

grid_deg.description: Grid spacing in whole degrees. Default 1 (Copernicus GLO-30 tiling).

mapping.description: Inline value -> attribute table. Keys are integer pixel values written as JSON strings (object keys are always strings). Each value is a flat object of scalar properties that becomes Feature.Properties verbatim.

propertyNames.description: Integer pixel value with no leading zeros, optional leading minus.

attributes.description: Flat set of attribute properties for one pixel value. Scalars only; UTF-8 strings should be quoted in YAML to avoid the 'Norway problem' (no/yes/on becoming booleans).
